title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Mike Yastrzemski's 10th inning single gives Braves a 3-2 victory over Red Sox.
Red Sox fell to a 18-26 record after leaving seven men on base.
Connelly Early pitched five innings, allowing two runs and striking out six for the Red Sox.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: The Patriots' potential trade for A.J. Brown is a primary reason a reunion with Stefon Diggs is unlikely.
Stefon Diggs expressed interest in returning, but salary cap issues make it difficult to accommodate him and Brown.
Stefon Diggs had a 1,045-yard season with the Patriots, but re-signing him would mean accepting a third receiver role.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston police are searching for Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes, a 15-year-old missing boy last seen Tuesday near Blue Hill Avenue, Dorchester.
LaGrant-Gomes is a Black male, 5'8", thin build, with brown eyes and black hair; he frequents areas like Columbia Road and Downtown Crossing.
Authorities urge anyone with information to contact Boston Police District B-3 detectives or submit anonymous tips through Boston Police CrimeStoppers.

title: Police: Teen allegedly shot girlfriend’s mother in the head on Mother’s Day
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: 18-year-old Tyler Broomes arrested for allegedly shooting his girlfriend's mother in Dorchester on Mother's Day.
The 42-year-old female victim hospitalized with life-threatening injuries; another man, also 42, had non-life-threatening wounds.
Broomes faces multiple charges, including assault with intent to murder; held without bail pending a May 21 hearing.

title: How would a team made up entirely of former Red Sox look in 2026?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Five former Red Sox pitchers, including Chris Sale and Nathan Eovaldi, have a combined 2.74 ERA in 2026.
Former Red Sox players Mookie Betts and Xander Bogaerts continue strong performances in 2026 with their current teams.
Kyle Schwarber leads MLB in home runs and was a former Red Sox free agent, but Boston did not pursue him.

title: Red Sox’ Roman Anthony no longer wearing wrist brace, played catch Friday
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox player Roman Anthony is recovering from a right wrist sprain, missing nine games since May 7.
Anthony has removed his wrist brace and played catch, but hasn't resumed batting yet as of Friday.
Interim manager Chad Tracy is hopeful Anthony will start batting soon, depending on his recovery progress.

title: Jeep drives into Norwood day care
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A Jeep accidentally crashed into BrightPath Norwood Child Care Center; no injuries were reported.
Emergency crews responded to the center on Providence Highway, assessing moderate structural damage.
The daycare was closed for further structural evaluation; the crash remains under investigation.

title: Here’s what experts are predicting the Patriots’ 2026 record will be
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Experts predict a 10-7 regular-season record for the New England Patriots in 2026, down from last season's 14-3.
The Patriots face a challenging schedule, including games against the Seahawks, Broncos, and Bears, affecting their projected wins.
Sports Illustrated offers the lowest prediction for the Patriots' 2026 record at 9-8, considering off-field controversies and a tough schedule.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Mars Attacks!' (1996) is streaming on YouTube and Plex, featuring Martin Short as a comedic White House press secretary.
'Marty, Life is Short' documentary featuring Martin Short, Steve Martin, and others is available on Netflix.
'Pop Culture Jeopardy!' Season 2, hosted by Colin Jost, has moved to Netflix with duo teams format.

title: Mayor Wu remains firm as councilors consider rejecting her budget
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu stands firm on her $4.9 billion budget despite City Council pushback over cuts.
City Council must decide on Wu's budget by June 10; they can amend line items, but not increase spending.
The city faces a $50 million deficit from expenses like snow removal and police overtime, prompting budget cuts.
